title: StarCoder
description: An open code model from the BigCode project that made permissive-code training and fill-in-the-middle behavior central to its identity.
openingSummary: StarCoder matters because it gave the code-model space a recognizable open reference point built around source code itself rather than around a general chat brand. The key idea for readers is that it is still a decoder-style language model, but its training mix, fill-in-the-middle focus, and code-centered ecosystem make it easier to discuss as a developer tool.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: StarCoder is a large language model for code released through the BigCode collaboration. In this atlas it serves as a practical checkpoint page for readers who want a named open code model that is distinct from Llama-based lines.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: StarCoder accepts tokenized prompts containing code, comments, and natural-language instructions, then predicts the next tokens needed to continue or complete the sequence. In coding workflows that often means code completion, missing-span insertion, and patch-style edits rather than ordinary paragraph continuation.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: The broad shape is still decoder-only and autoregressive, but StarCoder is especially useful for illustrating that code models can keep the same generation pattern while changing the objective and context shape. It also gives readers a concrete example of multi-query attention inside a coding model rather than only in a general-language one.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: Attention remains the basic context-sharing mechanism, and multi-query attention is worth linking here because it changes the serving tradeoff for a model that may sit behind many completion requests. The module story should stay grounded in how the system handles long code context, not in benchmark-score comparisons.

training.title: Training
training.body: Public StarCoder descriptions focus on large-scale training over permissively licensed code and a strong fill-in-the-middle story. This atlas slice still treats structured dataset and training-regime links as optional metadata, so the page keeps those empty states visible instead of hiding the missing records.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: StarCoder helps readers separate three ideas that often get blurred together: open code data policy, code completion behavior, and general language-model architecture. It belongs beside the code-repair-models category because the coding specialization matters more here than any one leaderboard snapshot.
